name: OperationalDesignDomain
desc: The complete set of conditions under which the automated driving system is designed to operate.
extend_desc: A sunny afternoon on a multi-lane urban highway, with moderate traffic flow and clear lane markings, within the vehicle's operational design domain for highway driving.
Dense fog reduces visibility to 50 meters on a rural two-lane road at dawn, challenging the perception system's operational limits in adverse weather conditions.
Heavy rain at night on a well-lit city street with reflective road surfaces, testing the sensor suite's performance under combined low-light and precipitation conditions.
A complex urban intersection during evening rush hour with multiple traffic signals, pedestrian crossings, and cyclists, within the defined ODD for city navigation.
Dry asphalt on a clear mountain pass road with sharp curves and elevation changes, operating within the system's validated road geometry parameters.
Light snowfall during daytime in a residential area with parked vehicles on both sides, evaluating performance in winter conditions within specified temperature ranges.
A construction zone on a suburban arterial road with temporary lane markings and reduced speed limits, within the ODD for road work navigation.
A well-maintained parking garage with concrete pillars and artificial lighting at midday, operating within the confined space parameters of the automated parking system.

Navigating a winding mountain highway during light snowfall, the road surface shows partial snow accumulation, guardrails are visible along steep drops, and reduced visibility requires cautious speed management.
Autonomous vehicle testing in a controlled industrial zone with minimal traffic, featuring wide lanes, simple intersections, and clear signage under consistent overcast daylight conditions.
Driving through a residential neighborhood at dusk with parked cars lining both sides of the street, children's play areas visible nearby, and speed bumps requiring reduced speed navigation.
Heavy rain conditions on a highway during daytime, with windshield wipers at maximum speed, water spray from surrounding vehicles reducing visibility, and wet road surfaces reflecting vehicle lights.

Approaching a construction zone on an urban arterial road, with orange cones redirecting traffic into narrowed lanes, temporary signage, and construction equipment partially visible in adjacent closed lanes.

In dense fog during early morning hours, the car navigates a bridge where visibility drops to less than 50 meters, with other vehicles' taillights appearing as diffuse red halos in the mist.

During a snowstorm on a rural highway, the autonomous system maintains traction control while following a snowplow's path, adjusting speed according to reduced visibility conditions.
The driverless vehicle merges into high-speed freeway traffic during morning commute, accurately predicting truck trajectories and finding safe gaps in the flow of vehicles.
In a construction zone with temporary lane markings and orange cones, the autonomous car successfully navigates the altered path while obeying reduced speed limits and worker presence.

In a busy downtown area with double-parked delivery trucks and jaywalking pedestrians, the autonomous car performs defensive driving maneuvers including emergency stops and obstacle avoidance.

desc: The complete set of conditions under which the automated driving system is designed to operate.

The perception system processes a complex roundabout entry during morning rush hour, identifying yield signs, pedestrian crossings, and continuous traffic flow from the left requiring gap analysis.
At night on an unlit country road, the infrared-enhanced camera detects a deer standing near the roadside beyond the headlight range, with limited environmental context due to darkness.

During snowfall, the camera fusion system tracks lane boundaries through partially obscured markings while identifying slowing traffic patterns ahead due to reduced traction conditions on this bridge crossing.

The multi-camera array perceives a construction zone with orange cones redirecting traffic, temporary signage indicating lane shifts, and workers present near the active work area on this highway section.

In dense urban traffic during evening hours, the perception stack identifies multiple vulnerable road users including cyclists in bike lanes, jaywalking pedestrians, and double-parked delivery vehicles obstructing partial lanes.

name: Cloudy
desc: Overall atmospheric conditions at the time of driving.
extend_desc: The vehicle drives through a suburban highway under a thick overcast sky, with scattered cumulus clouds hanging low at approximately 1,500 feet, casting a uniform diffused light that eliminates harsh shadows on the four-lane asphalt.

In a residential area during the morning commute, the car moves along a tree-lined street under a complete overcast (eight oktas) of nimbostratus clouds, resulting in a flat, gray illumination that makes colors appear muted, with light traffic ahead.

The vehicle ascends a hill on a rural road, with the sky showing broken cloud cover (five oktas) of cirrostratus high above, allowing intermittent sunlight to create shifting light patterns on the two-lane road, enhancing the depth perception of the landscape.

On a coastal highway, the car travels with a view of the ocean under a sky of four oktas of cumulus clouds at varying heights, the illumination bright but without direct glare, perfect for clear visibility of the curving road and sparse traffic.

In an urban canyon between skyscrapers, the vehicle approaches an intersection under a uniform layer of stratus clouds (eight oktas) at a low base, creating a dull, shadowless light that evenly illuminates the dense traffic and pedestrian crosswalks.

name: Night
desc: Lighting conditions affecting visibility and sensor performance.
extend_desc: A vehicle navigates a winding mountain road under a full moon, the lunar illumination casting sharp shadows of pine trees across the asphalt, with sparse artificial lights from distant cabins visible in the valleys below.

Driving through a poorly lit suburban street on a moonless night, the headlights cut through the deep darkness, revealing parked cars and the occasional glow from a house window after the driver's eyes have fully dark-adapted.
The car proceeds down a bustling downtown avenue at midnight, bathed in the dense, overlapping artificial glow of neon signs, street lamps, and building facades, obscuring any view of the night sky.

On a clear, new moon night in the rural countryside, the vehicle's path is faintly outlined by the exceptional visibility of the Milky Way and starlight, with no other artificial light sources for miles.

Approaching a highway interchange at dusk, the transition into night is gradual; the last amber of sunset fades as urban skyglow and countless headlights/taillights dominate the scene, requiring minimal dark adaptation.

A delivery van drives through an industrial district's service road, where intermittent, harsh high-pressure sodium vapor lamps create pools of orange light separated by stretches of deep shadow under a gibbous moon.

During a late-night commute on a wet urban bridge, the asphalt reflects the chaotic tapestry of city lights and red brake lights from moderate traffic, with a hazy crescent moon barely visible through light pollution.

An autonomous vehicle sensors capture a residential neighborhood scene after midnight, where minimal porch lights and a bright quarter moon provide just enough illumination to discern sidewalks and parked vehicle silhouettes.

name: ArtificialLight
desc: Lighting conditions affecting visibility and sensor performance.
extend_desc: At a highway toll plaza at night, high-pressure sodium lamps cast warm orange light over multiple lanes, creating sharp shadows from the gantry structure while vehicles' headlights add moving light streaks.
Driving through a downtown tunnel during evening rush hour, bright white LED panels mounted on the ceiling provide uniform illumination, reflecting off wet pavement as sedans maintain steady speeds.

Approaching a suburban intersection after sunset, flickering mercury-vapor streetlights create uneven pools of bluish light on the road surface while traffic signals add intermittent color variations.

In a residential area during heavy rain at night, distant yellow incandescent porch lights create hazy halos through the precipitation, with occasional carport fluorescents casting sharp geometric shadows.
Passing under a railway bridge at dusk, cool-white LED floodlights mounted on the structure create stark contrast zones between illuminated concrete surfaces and deep shadow areas beneath.
On a rural highway during foggy conditions, widely spaced high-mast lighting with metal-halide lamps creates visible light cones in the atmosphere, with intensity decreasing between poles.
At a commercial district parking lot entrance, multicolored LED advertising displays create dynamic color shifts on nearby vehicles while standard streetlights maintain baseline white illumination.

Driving through an industrial zone after midnight, sporadic high-intensity discharge lamps from factory yards create isolated bright zones amid generally dark roads, with occasional security lights activating motion sensors.

name: NoPrecipitation

Dust lightly rises from a gravel roadside as a vehicle passes by on a completely dry rural road during midday.
The vehicle's windshield remains clean without wiper marks, indicating no recent rain on this urban street with moderate traffic.
Sunlight reflects sharply off the parched concrete highway, with visible heat haze shimmering above the bone-dry road surface.
A delivery truck kicks up small dust clouds while turning onto a dry industrial zone road with cracked pavement edges.
Evening commute on a moisture-free freeway, with dry tire tracks visible on lanes and no spray from surrounding vehicles.
Parked cars cast crisp shadows on completely dry residential street asphalt after a full day without precipitation.
Construction zone on a desert highway with dry, powdery dirt accumulating on the road shoulders and no mud patches anywhere.

Light drizzle persists through the night shift, creating subtle reflections of streetlights and neon signs on the glistening city streets, with wipers occasionally clearing fine mist from the camera lens.

Post-storm residual rainfall creates variable road conditions during morning hours, with some lanes showing standing water patches while others merely appear damp, requiring careful lane selection by the autonomous system.
Freezing rain begins during evening rush hour, creating a hazardous sheen on the overpass surface that reflects headlights in distorted patterns, with wipers struggling against ice accumulation.

Monsoon-intensity rainfall reduces visibility to under 50 meters on the coastal highway during afternoon hours, creating complete whiteout conditions between wiper strokes as the system relies heavily on radar sensors.
Intermittent showers alternate with clear periods during a rural route at midday, creating rapidly changing road friction coefficients as the sun dries some sections while others remain slick.

Urban street flooding occurs after prolonged heavy rainfall, creating bow waves from passing buses that temporarily obscure all camera vision, requiring emergency slowdown protocols until water clears.

extend_desc: A green traffic light at an urban intersection during daytime, clearly visible ahead with a 15-second countdown, allowing the ego vehicle to proceed straight through moderate traffic.
At dusk, a distant red light at a T-junction is partially occluded by a large truck in the adjacent lane, its countdown timer unclear due to the low angle of the sun.
A yellow light begins flashing at a pedestrian crossing on a suburban road at night, signaling caution as a few vehicles slow down in the light rain.
A green arrow traffic light for a left-turn lane at a major intersection, visible under overcast daylight, shows a 5-second countdown with steady illumination.
A red light at a near-side intersection during rush hour, fully visible with a 30-second countdown, while multiple cars and a motorcycle wait in line.
A traffic light at a highway off-ramp junction, showing a solid green light with no countdown, clearly visible under bright midday sun with light traffic flow.
At night, a traffic light at a four-way stop displays a steady red light, its face slightly glared by the headlights of oncoming vehicles in the opposite lane.
A yellow traffic light at a city intersection during light rain, positioned at the far end, begins its countdown from 3 seconds as the ego vehicle prepares to stop.

name: TrafficSign
desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.
extend_desc: A large yellow warning sign with a sharp turn symbol appears ahead on a mountain road, its reflective surface clearly visible under afternoon sunlight.
At a busy urban intersection, a blue instruction sign indicating a pedestrian crossing stands beside the road, slightly worn but still legible during morning rush hour.
A circular prohibition sign with a red border and speed limit '40' is mounted on a roadside pole, partially obscured by tree branches on this suburban street.
On a highway during light rain at dusk, a rectangular advance direction sign with green background shows clear reflective properties, guiding vehicles toward upcoming exits.
A triangular warning sign depicting falling rocks is positioned before a tunnel entrance, showing moderate wear but remaining clearly visible under overcast daylight conditions.
In dense city traffic, a temporary orange construction warning sign with diagonal stripes stands near roadwork, its fluorescent coating noticeable in midday sun.
A small regulatory sign indicating 'No Parking' is attached to a building wall at a narrow alley intersection, requiring close attention due to its compact dimensions.
On a rural road at dawn, a distance marker sign shows kilometers to next town, with high-grade reflectivity making it readable in low-light conditions.

name: GuardRail
desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.
extend_desc: A W-beam guardrail runs along the right shoulder of a two-lane rural highway, its silver surface reflecting the afternoon sun, with minor surface rust visible near the base.
At the entrance to a highway tunnel, a concrete safety barrier with a red reflective end treatment curves away from the roadway, guiding traffic into the dimly lit bore.
A damaged section of steel cable guardrail on a mountain road's sharp curve shows signs of a recent impact, with several posts bent and cables sagging.
On a wet, four-lane urban arterial after rain, a long stretch of galvanized steel box-beam guardrail separates opposing traffic, its surface glistening under streetlights.
A short, sand-filled plastic crash cushion (end treatment) is installed in front of a concrete bridge abutment at a freeway off-ramp, its bright yellow color highly visible.
Along a winding coastal road, a continuous concrete barrier with horizontal scoring follows the cliff's edge, showing weathered patches but otherwise intact.
At a highway construction zone, a temporary, movable concrete barrier separates live traffic from the work area, with orange and white striping for high visibility.
On a snow-covered interstate, a long run of strong-post W-beam guardrail is partially obscured by plowed snow, but the top rail and reflective markers remain clear.

name: Barrier
desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.
extend_desc: A temporary orange plastic barrier separates the left lane for construction work on a suburban highway, with reflective stripes visible under morning sunlight.
Concrete Jersey barriers line the center median of a wet urban expressway at dusk, preventing crossover accidents with oncoming traffic.
A movable steel guardrail blocks the right turn lane during evening rush hour, with flashing arrow boards redirecting vehicles to detour.
Low-profile rubber speed bumps act as traffic calming barriers in a residential area, with a car ahead slowly crossing them under clear afternoon light.
A row of sturdy metal bollards protects the pedestrian plaza entrance from vehicle intrusion, visible from the car's perspective while waiting at an adjacent intersection.
Tall wire mesh fencing serves as a noise barrier along the highway, with morning shadows cast across its surface as the vehicle drives past in moderate traffic.
Plastic water-filled barriers delineate a temporary lane shift on a bridge, their yellow caps reflecting the vehicle's headlights during night-time construction.
A retractable belt barrier blocks the parking garage exit, with a 'Do Not Enter' sign attached, viewed from the approaching vehicle's dashboard camera in light rain.

name: Crosswalk
desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.
extend_desc: A wide crosswalk with faded zebra stripes stretches across a suburban road during morning rush hour, with a steady stream of pedestrians crossing while vehicles wait patiently at the stop line.

At a busy urban intersection, a narrow crosswalk is clearly marked with fresh white paint, protected by pedestrian traffic signals that currently show a red hand, halting foot traffic as cars pass through.
An unmarked crosswalk at a residential street corner during light rain, where a few pedestrians are cautiously crossing despite the lack of formal zebra stripes, requiring the vehicle to yield.

A crosswalk equipped with a raised safety island in the middle divides the crossing into two stages, allowing pedestrians to pause safely while navigating a wide, multi-lane arterial road during midday.
At night, a poorly lit crosswalk with worn-out markings appears ahead under streetlights, with a lone pedestrian wearing reflective clothing starting to cross, prompting the vehicle to slow down.

A crosswalk at a school zone during dismissal hours, featuring bright yellow zebra stripes and flashing warning lights, with a crossing guard holding a stop sign to assist children crossing in groups.

An empty crosswalk across a quiet neighborhood street on a weekend afternoon, with clear zebra markings and no pedestrian activity, allowing the vehicle to proceed after confirming no one is approaching.

A crosswalk at a T-intersection during moderate pedestrian flow, where some individuals are crossing against the signal while others wait, creating a mixed scenario that requires careful attention and potential yielding.

name: RailroadCrossing
desc: Fixed physical structures supporting road safety and control.
extend_desc: Approaching a single-track rural railroad crossing at dusk, the red warning lights flash rhythmically while the crossing gates remain fully raised, with no train in sight on the distant tracks.
A heavy freight train passes through a double-track urban railroad crossing during midday, the crossing gates are fully lowered, and the warning bells ring loudly as vehicles queue on both sides.
Stopping at a modern railroad crossing with four parallel tracks, the automated gates begin descending as alternating red lights flash, indicating an approaching high-speed passenger train.

Waiting at a rarely-used industrial spur crossing at night, the warning equipment appears inactive and the gates are fixed in the upright position, requiring careful visual scanning of both directions.

A commuter train swiftly crosses a suburban railroad crossing during morning rush hour, the gates have just started rising after the train passed, and queued vehicles begin accelerating across the tracks.
At a historic single-track crossing in a small town, the manual crossing gates are being lowered by an attendant, while the classic warning bell clangs steadily as a local train approaches.
Heavy rain obscures visibility at a multi-track crossing near a freight yard, the red warning lights create reflective streaks on the wet pavement as multiple locomotives slowly pass through.

During autumn evening, vehicles line up at a forest-lined railroad crossing, the crossing gates are down and warning lights flash, but the expected train has been delayed causing extended waiting time.

name: TimeOfDay
desc: Time-related factors affecting traffic and environment.
extend_desc: A vehicle-mounted camera captures the morning rush hour at 7:30 AM, with soft golden-hour sunlight casting long shadows across the wet asphalt after overnight rain, commuter traffic steadily flowing through suburban arterials.

Midday driving scenario at 1:15 PM shows harsh overhead sunlight creating strong contrast on the highway, minimal traffic during off-peak hours, with visible heat shimmer rising from the pavement in summer conditions.

Evening transition period at 6:45 PM during winter months, with deep blue twilight sky blending into artificial street lighting, increased pedestrian activity near commercial districts as businesses close.
Late night urban driving at 2:20 AM shows nearly empty six-lane boulevards illuminated by orange sodium-vapor lamps, occasional delivery vehicles and taxis visible in the distance.

Pre-dawn highway scenario at 5:10 AM captures the first hints of sunrise coloring the eastern horizon, sparse traffic consisting mainly of long-haul trucks, with dew still visible on roadside vegetation.

Afternoon school zone at 3:05 PM shows heightened activity with parents picking up children, dappled sunlight filtering through mature trees lining residential streets, crossing guards assisting pedestrians.

Golden hour commute at 5:50 PM during autumn, with low-angle sunlight creating lens flare across the windshield, moderate-to-heavy traffic moving through downtown corridors as office workers head home.

Midnight industrial zone navigation at 11:40 PM shows isolated roadways with patchy fog developing, minimal lighting except for security lamps at warehouse facilities, requiring careful speed adjustment.

name: Season
desc: Time-related factors affecting traffic and environment.
extend_desc: A winter morning scene with light snow falling, the road surface covered in a thin layer of fresh snow, and vehicles moving slowly with headlights on in the dim dawn light.
A bright summer afternoon on a suburban road, with strong sunlight creating sharp shadows, dry asphalt shimmering with heat haze, and sparse traffic under clear blue skies.
Autumn evening commute with wet leaves covering road markings after afternoon rain, windshield wipers intermittently clearing drizzle, and dense traffic with glowing brake lights in the fading light.
Spring midday in a residential area with moderate sunshine filtering through new foliage, puddles remaining from morning showers, and children playing near the curb as cars navigate cautiously.
Deep winter blizzard conditions with heavy snow accumulation on roads, reduced visibility through swirling snowflakes, and vehicles maintaining large following distances with hazard lights activated.
Late summer monsoon season with torrential rain creating road spray, wipers at maximum speed struggling to maintain visibility, and vehicles crawling through flooded intersections.
Crisp autumn morning with frost glazing the road surface, sun glare reflecting off icy patches, and commuter traffic moving carefully with increased stopping distances.

Spring dawn during cherry blossom season with petals drifting across wet pavement after overnight rain, gentle morning light filtering through flowering trees, and light traffic beginning the day's flow.

name: Holiday
desc: Time-related factors affecting traffic and environment.
extend_desc: During the National Day holiday, vehicles move slowly on the city's main road, with dense traffic flow and many cars carrying luggage, as families travel for the holiday.
On the morning of New Year's Day, the vehicle drives on a suburban highway, with noticeably lighter traffic than usual, and occasional cars passing by, enjoying the relaxed holiday atmosphere.
At the start of the Spring Festival travel rush, the vehicle approaches a highway toll station, with long queues of cars waiting to pass, and electronic signs displaying holiday traffic reminders.

During the Mid-Autumn Festival holiday evening, the vehicle travels through a commercial district, with crowded sidewalks filled with pedestrians shopping and celebrating, while traffic remains orderly but slow-moving.
On a weekend holiday, the vehicle navigates a scenic mountain road, encountering intermittent clusters of tourist vehicles, with some cars parked on the roadside for sightseeing.

During the Labor Day holiday, the vehicle passes by an amusement park entrance, where temporary traffic control measures are in place, with police directing traffic and vehicles moving in designated lanes.
On Christmas night, the vehicle drives through the city center, with streets decorated in holiday themes, increased pedestrian crossings, and traffic flow fluctuating as people attend celebrations.

During the Tomb-Sweeping Festival, the vehicle travels on a rural road leading to cemetery areas, with a noticeable increase in vehicle flow, and some cars parked along the roadside for family gatherings.

name: SpecialEvent
desc: Time-related factors affecting traffic and environment.
extend_desc: A large-scale music festival is concluding, causing hundreds of pedestrians to spill onto the adjacent urban avenue, with temporary traffic cones and police officers redirecting vehicle flow onto a single lane for approximately 20 minutes.

A minor parade celebrating a local holiday passes through a suburban commercial street, featuring floats and a marching band, with light to moderate pedestrian crowds gathered on sidewalks, and traffic is halted intermittently for 3-5 minutes at a time.

Emergency responders handle a multi-vehicle collision on a highway overpass, creating a significant traffic bottleneck; two right lanes are closed with flares and signage, and the duration of the closure is expected to be around 45 minutes.

A film crew has secured a two-block section of a downtown street for shooting, with equipment, trailers, and crew members partially occupying the roadway; traffic is diverted via detour signs, and controlled access is maintained for local residents over a 6-hour period.

A spontaneous street protest with a dense crowd of demonstrators occupies the main thoroughfare, moving slowly and chanting; police vehicles with flashing lights block intersections ahead, and vehicle traffic is at a complete standstill for an unpredictable duration.

A weekend street fair transforms a residential boulevard, with vendor booths lining the curbs and moderate pedestrian flow crossing between them; the road is reduced to one-way traffic with a lowered speed limit, controlled by periodic flaggers throughout the day.

A charity marathon is in progress on a coastal parkway, with runners occupying the outer lane; traffic is merged into the inner lane with clear signage and periodic race marshals, creating light congestion for the event's 3-hour duration.

A sudden, localized heavy downpour causes flash flooding on a low-lying urban street, with water covering half the lane depth; emergency services have set up roadblocks and are diverting all traffic, with no estimated time for reopening.

name: SpeedLimits
desc: Laws and regulations governing driving in the region.

Temporary construction zone speed limit of 30 km/h is enforced with portable radar display showing current vehicle speeds on a four-lane arterial road.

Minimum speed limit of 60 km/h is posted alongside maximum 100 km/h on a freeway during clear afternoon conditions, with traffic flowing smoothly in all lanes.
School bus speed limit of 20 km/h is active as a bus with flashing red lights stops ahead, with all lanes of traffic coming to a complete stop.

name: RightOfWayRules
desc: Laws and regulations governing driving in the region.
extend_desc: A vehicle approaches a four-way stop intersection, with the car to the right arriving first, establishing clear right-of-way priority.
At a T-junction, the main road vehicle maintains right-of-way while a side road car waits for a safe gap in traffic.
During merging onto a highway, the vehicle yields to existing traffic flow, adjusting speed to match the acceleration lane.
At an uncontrolled intersection with equal approach, the vehicle on the left yields to the vehicle on the right according to basic right-of-way rules.
When encountering emergency vehicles with activated lights and sirens, all traffic yields right-of-way by pulling to the right side of the road.
At a roundabout, entering vehicles yield to circulating traffic, waiting for a sufficient gap before merging into the flow.
When turning left at a green light without a protected arrow, the vehicle yields to oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk.
At a pedestrian crosswalk without traffic signals, the vehicle stops completely as a pedestrian steps onto the roadway, granting them right-of-way.

On a three-lane freeway with light traffic, the ego vehicle uses the leftmost lane to overtake a slower truck, then signals to return to the center lane after completing the pass.
During heavy rain on a suburban arterial road, the vehicle refrains from overtaking a cyclist despite a dashed line, due to reduced visibility and wet road conditions.
When approaching a school zone during operational hours with flashing lights, all vehicles maintain speed and lane discipline without any overtaking maneuvers, as per special section rules.
On a highway with a climbing lane for slow vehicles, the ego vehicle properly uses the designated lane to overtake heavy trucks, then merges back smoothly into the main lane.

In a commercial district during evening hours, the vehicle passes angled parking spaces along the curb, noting a digital payment station with illuminated instructions.
The car slowly drives through a hospital parking lot, observing time-limited parking zones with 30-minute maximum signs and color-coded sections for different purposes.
At a suburban train station, the vehicle enters a park-and-ride facility, noting electronic guidance signs directing to available perpendicular parking spots in specific rows.
During daytime in a historic district, the car passes narrow streets with alternating side parking permissions indicated by dated signs specifying allowed days.
The vehicle approaches a loading zone marked with yellow curb paint, observing commercial delivery vehicles using the space within permitted hours as shown on adjacent signage.

name: RegionalLegislation
desc: Laws and regulations governing driving in the region.
extend_desc: A vehicle-mounted camera in Germany captures the right lane on an autobahn without speed limits, showing a luxury sedan overtaking at high speed while a truck maintains its lane in accordance with local regulations.
In Japan, a first-person view shows the vehicle stopping completely at a railroad crossing with lowered gates, as required by strict traffic laws, with red warning lights flashing in the rain.

Approaching a school zone in California, USA, the view shows the vehicle slowing to 25 mph as required by flashing beacons, with strict enforcement signs prominently displayed.

In Singapore, the vehicle-mounted view shows electronic road pricing gantries deducting tolls automatically, reflecting the city-state's technology-driven regulatory enforcement system.

name: AutonomyLevel
desc: Performance and functional limits of the subject vehicle.
extend_desc: A driver monitors the road while hands remain on the steering wheel of an L2 vehicle, which maintains lane centering on a clear highway during light traffic.
From the vehicle's dashboard camera view, the system prompts for driver takeover as it approaches the boundary of its operational design domain on a rural, unmarked road.
In heavy urban traffic, an L3 autonomous vehicle navigates a complex intersection with traffic lights and pedestrians, with the driver reading a book but ready to intervene.
A fully autonomous L5 vehicle with no steering wheel maneuvers smoothly through a dense, rainy downtown area at night, handling all perception and decision-making tasks.
An L1 vehicle with adaptive cruise control follows a truck on a highway, but the driver performs all steering inputs during a moderate curve in the road.
During a sudden lane closure on a freeway, an L4 robotaxi with redundant sensor systems executes a safe lane change without any passenger input, captured from the front-facing camera.
At dusk on a suburban street, an L2+ system assists with lane keeping and speed control, but the driver must constantly supervise as cyclists share the roadway.
Inside a vehicle with conditional automation (L3), the system issues a warning as it encounters a severe snowstorm beyond its ODD, requiring the driver to resume control immediately.

name: SensorSuite
desc: Performance and functional limits of the subject vehicle.
extend_desc: The vehicle's sensor suite activates in dense morning fog, with LiDAR providing sparse point clouds while radar maintains reliable object detection through the obscuration.
On a sun-drenched highway at noon, the camera system's dynamic range is challenged by extreme contrast, while redundant ultrasonic sensors handle close-range parking maneuvers.
During heavy rainfall at dusk, the forward-facing millimeter-wave radar continues tracking vehicles ahead as camera visibility degrades significantly due to water droplets on lenses.
In urban canyon environments with tall buildings, GPS signals become intermittent while the inertial measurement unit and visual odometry maintain localization continuity.
The sensor fusion system correlates data from eight cameras, five radars, and one LiDAR to construct a 360-degree environmental model during complex intersection navigation.
At night on an unlit country road, thermal imaging detects wildlife beyond the headlight range while LiDAR precisely maps the road edges invisible to cameras.
During winter snow conditions, the heated sensor array maintains operation while algorithms compensate for radar reflections off snowflakes and camera glare from wet surfaces.
In stop-and-go traffic with multiple vehicle types, the sensor suite's classification algorithms distinguish between motorcycles, sedans, and trucks using combined camera and LiDAR signatures.

name: ActuatorPerformance
desc: Performance and functional limits of the subject vehicle.
extend_desc: The steering actuator responds with minimal latency during a sharp turn on a winding mountain road, maintaining precise control accuracy despite the challenging terrain.

At a busy urban intersection during rush hour, the brake actuator demonstrates high fault tolerance by smoothly decelerating when a pedestrian suddenly crosses, with redundancy systems ready to engage.
On a wet highway during heavy rain, the throttle actuator shows excellent control accuracy by maintaining consistent speed and traction, with response latency under 50ms for adaptive adjustments.
During an emergency lane change on a crowded freeway, the steering actuator's quick response latency of under 30ms allows the vehicle to avoid a collision while staying within the execution range.
The brake actuator's redundancy design is tested when descending a steep hill, with primary and backup systems working in tandem to maintain safe deceleration control.
In stop-and-go traffic on a city street, the throttle actuator demonstrates precise control accuracy with minimal overshoot, smoothly accelerating and decelerating within tight execution ranges.
When navigating through a construction zone with uneven road surfaces, the suspension actuators show high fault tolerance by adapting to unexpected bumps while maintaining vehicle stability.
During high-speed highway merging at night, the steering actuator's low latency response and wide execution range enable seamless lane changes despite reduced visibility conditions.

name: Communication
desc: Performance and functional limits of the subject vehicle.
extend_desc: The vehicle's 5G-V2X system maintains stable communication with roadside units while navigating through a downtown arterial road during morning rush hour, with latency below 20ms ensuring real-time traffic signal phase reception.

In a suburban highway scenario with moderate traffic density, the autonomous vehicle utilizes DSRC technology to exchange basic safety messages with nearby trucks, achieving 95% packet delivery reliability within 300-meter coverage.

During heavy rain on an urban expressway at night, the vehicle's multi-mode communication system switches between 5G and LTE-V2X links to maintain minimum 10Mbps bandwidth for high-definition map updates.

Approaching a complex multi-level interchange, the autonomous driving system receives cooperative perception data from three preceding vehicles via C-V2X, with end-to-end latency controlled within 100ms for merging decision-making.

In a tunnel section with partial communication coverage, the vehicle employs store-carry-forward mechanisms to relay emergency brake warnings from ahead, maintaining 80% communication reliability despite intermittent connectivity.

During peak hours on a smart highway corridor, the ego vehicle participates in platoon coordination using 5G-NR sidelink communication, achieving millimeter-wave level positioning synchronization with lead vehicle.

At a signalized intersection with dense pedestrian traffic, the V2I system receives SPAT messages from roadside infrastructure with 99.9% reliability, enabling precise green light optimal speed advisory.

While navigating through an urban canyon area with high-rise buildings, the autonomous vehicle dynamically adjusts communication power and frequency to mitigate multi-path fading effects, maintaining continuous V2N connectivity.

name: VisibilityRange
desc: System ability to detect and recognize objects and features.
extend_desc: A clear sunny day on a straight highway, visibility extends beyond 500 meters with distant vehicles clearly discernible and road markings sharply defined.
Dense fog reduces visibility to approximately 50 meters on a rural road, with only the tail lights of the immediately preceding car faintly visible through the mist.
Heavy rainfall at dusk on an urban avenue, visibility limited to about 100 meters with wipers at high speed and surrounding vehicles appearing as blurred silhouettes.
Sudden snow squall on a mountain pass, visibility drops to under 30 meters with road edges completely obscured and only faint outlines of guardrails detectable.
Nighttime driving on an unlit country road, high beams illuminate about 150 meters ahead while peripheral areas fade into complete darkness beyond the light cone.
Sandstorm conditions on a desert highway, visibility reduced to 20 meters with horizontal particulate occlusion making distant terrain features indistinguishable.
Morning coastal fog partially obscuring a bridge crossing, visibility fluctuates between 80-200 meters as fog patches drift across the roadway.
Urban tunnel exit at midday, temporary blindness occurs as eyes adjust from dark to bright, with visibility recovery taking 3-5 seconds before full scene perception returns.

name: ObstacleDetection
desc: System ability to detect and recognize objects and features.
extend_desc: The vehicle-mounted camera detects a stationary cardboard box 15 meters ahead on a suburban road during daytime, with the system displaying 95% confidence and responding within 100 milliseconds.
In heavy urban traffic at dusk, the autonomous system identifies a pedestrian suddenly crossing 8 meters ahead with 85% confidence, triggering emergency braking within 150 milliseconds.
On a foggy highway, the system struggles to detect a broken-down truck 30 meters ahead, showing only 60% confidence and a 200-millisecond delayed response.
During nighttime rural driving, the camera clearly captures a deer standing 20 meters ahead on the road, with 98% detection confidence and immediate 50-millisecond response.
In moderate rain on a city street, the system intermittently detects a bicycle 10 meters ahead, showing fluctuating confidence between 70-90% and occasional missed detections.
Approaching a construction zone at noon, the vehicle detects multiple traffic cones 25 meters ahead with 99% confidence, planning avoidance within 80 milliseconds.
In dense urban traffic during rush hour, the system fails to detect a small motorcycle cutting in from the side at 5 meters distance, representing a missed detection scenario.
On a clear highway curve, the camera identifies an overturned cart 40 meters ahead with 92% confidence, allowing smooth lane change planning within 120 milliseconds.

name: LaneRecognition
desc: System ability to detect and recognize objects and features.
extend_desc: The vehicle's forward-facing camera clearly captures the white dashed lane markings on a straight highway segment under bright midday sunlight, maintaining accurate lane tracking at 100 meters with minimal curvature.

During heavy rain at dusk, the windshield wipers struggle to keep up as the camera attempts to identify faded yellow lane lines on a wet urban road, with recognition accuracy dropping due to water reflections.

In a dense downtown area with multiple lane changes, the system successfully distinguishes between solid white, dashed white, and double yellow lines while navigating through moderate afternoon traffic.
Approaching a sharp mountain curve at night, the infrared-enhanced cameras detect lane boundaries despite limited visibility, though recognition distance is reduced to 40 meters on the winding road.

The autonomous system momentarily loses lane tracking when passing through a construction zone where temporary orange lane markings conflict with partially covered permanent white lines during morning commute.
On a newly paved suburban road under overcast conditions, the cameras precisely identify fresh thermoplastic lane markings with 95% accuracy while maintaining a 120-meter recognition range.

As the vehicle enters a long tunnel with artificial lighting, the system adapts to recognize reflective raised pavement markers alongside standard painted lines, updating lane position 30 times per second.

During autumn evening drive, fallen leaves partially obscure lane markings on a country road, causing intermittent recognition failures as the system attempts to filter out natural debris interference.

name: SignRecognition
desc: System ability to detect and recognize objects and features.
extend_desc: A vehicle-mounted camera captures a clear stop sign at 30 meters on a suburban road, with the system achieving 98% recognition accuracy within 200 milliseconds under moderate morning sunlight.
In heavy urban rain at dusk, the autonomous system struggles to recognize a faded speed limit sign at 25 meters, resulting in 85% accuracy and a 400ms response time due to poor visibility.

On a highway during clear noon conditions, the system instantly detects and classifies an overhead electronic message sign at 50 meters with 99.9% accuracy and 150ms response, displaying route guidance information.

In dense downtown traffic with multiple overlapping signs, the system prioritizes a right-turn-only sign at 15 meters but experiences a 5% false recognition rate due to visual clutter from advertisements.

Approaching a construction zone on a cloudy afternoon, the vehicle recognizes a temporary detour sign at 40 meters with 94% accuracy, though response time increases to 350ms due to non-standard sign formatting.

At a complex multi-lane interchange during sunrise glare, the autonomous system uses polarization filters to read an overhead exit sign at 60 meters, maintaining 96% accuracy despite challenging lighting conditions.

In snowy mountain pass conditions with partial sign occlusion, the system identifies a falling rocks warning sign at 18 meters with 88% accuracy, requiring 500ms processing time and showing occasional false alerts for similar shapes.

During highway merging, the map's centimeter-level relative accuracy provides precise lane alignment, allowing the ADAS to smoothly follow the curved on-ramp.
In this rural area with infrequent map updates, data aging causes significant absolute accuracy errors, showing the vehicle position offset from the actual unpaved road.
The digital map exhibits uniform error distribution across the downtown grid, with all street intersections consistently shifted by approximately 50 centimeters relative to real-world positions.
Fresh map data with weekly update frequency maintains excellent absolute accuracy, accurately depicting newly installed roundabout geometry and lane dividers in real-time.
